title: salix phylicifolia subsp. planifolia
intro: Salix phylicifolia subsp. planifolia, commonly known as the marsh willow or white willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree that grows in marshy areas and has leaves with a smooth, white underside. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which have been used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia subsp. planifolia include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating fever, rheumatism, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make salves, ointments, and teas for medicinal purposes.
